# @moku-labs/room

> Couch-multiplayer foundation for Moku: shared screen (stage) + phones (controllers), WebRTC peer-to-peer,
> multi-device state sync. A standalone Moku FRAMEWORK on `@moku-labs/core` — you `createApp` from it.

Room is its OWN `@moku-labs/core` framework — a sibling to `@moku-labs/web`/`@moku-labs/worker`, NOT built on them.
It defines one `createCoreConfig` (in `src/config.ts`), wires the four engines as core defaults, and EXPORTS the
bound `createApp` + `createPlugin`; the framework never calls `createApp` — Layer-3 apps do. `@moku-labs/core` and
`@moku-labs/common` (which supplies `ctx.log`/`ctx.env`) are BUNDLED dependencies, not peers — there is nothing extra
to install. A consumer adds a role facade (`stagePlugin` host / `controllerPlugin` phone) + its game plugin; there
are no role arrays.

- Package: `@moku-labs/room` (bundled deps `@moku-labs/core 1.5.0`, `@moku-labs/common 0.2.1`, `trystero ~0.25.2`,
  `qrcode ^1.5.4`). **No peer dependencies.** Engines: node ≥24, bun ≥1.3.14.
- Install: `bun add @moku-labs/room` (that's all — `createApp`/`createPlugin` come from Room itself).
- Entries: `@moku-labs/room` (`.`, the CLIENT core — `createApp` for the browser game; also runs node tests, so there
  is no separate browser entry) · `@moku-labs/room/server` (`./server`, the OPT-IN server core — `createApp` for a
  Cloudflare Worker signaling tier: `hubPlugin` + the `Hub` Durable Object).

## Core concepts

- **Stage vs controller.** Stage = the shared TV/laptop screen, the authoritative HOST and star hub: owns game
  state, validates inputs, broadcasts state, calls `createRoom()`. Controller = a phone, a passive peer: joins by
  code, sends typed intents, renders a read-only state replica.
- **Peer-to-peer, no game server (D2 — accepted hard-failure).** Strictly P2P, NO TURN ever. Stage `createRoom()`
  → 6-char code → QR → both devices join a public rendezvous (Trystero/Nostr by default) → one-time SDP/ICE
  handshake over public relays → direct P2P DataChannels on the LAN. The rendezvous never carries gameplay. On
  AP-isolated / symmetric-NAT / iOS-Private-Relay networks (~15–30%) the connection hard-fails with no recovery;
  surfaces `room:network-warning`. Design target: the home LAN.
- **Wire vs events (two planes, strictly separate).** The typed `Wire` (`Frame` DataChannel) carries ALL gameplay
  (intents, snapshots, deltas, heartbeats, recovery, roster). Moku `emit` (`room:*`) carries ONLY coarse lifecycle
  (5 events). No gameplay ever rides `emit`; no `room:*` event ever carries gameplay.
- **Star topology, 8-cap.** Stage is the sole hub; NO controller↔controller channels. `MAX_CONTROLLERS = 8`,
  `ROOM_CODE_LENGTH = 6`.
- **Signaling adapter seam (D12).** Transport talks to discovery only through the DOM-free `Signaling` contract.
  Adapters swap with zero transport changes: `publicRendezvous()` (default, Trystero), `inMemory()` (tests; pass
  `{ server: true }` to simulate the server protocol), and `serverSignaling(url)` (opt-in, worker-backed; lazy-loaded).
- **Optional operated signaling tier (D21/D25, `@moku-labs/room/server`).** An opt-in server core: a Cloudflare Worker
  with ONE Durable Object per room (`Hub`, WebSocket Hibernation). Reached via `serverSignaling(url)`. Brokers
  SIGNALING ONLY — handshake + in-band discovery + host-reload reclaim; NO gameplay relay, so **D2 still holds**
  (gameplay stays strict P2P, no TURN). The `hub` reaches the DO + KV + ASSETS through the per-request native
  Cloudflare `env` — there is no `@moku-labs/worker` dependency. A `serverSignaling` session is `persistent: true` —
  transport keeps the WS open past ICE as the discovery-push + reclaim conduit (vs. `publicRendezvous`/`inMemory`
  which discard the session once connected). The DO mints a `reclaimToken` on join → `session` persists it → replays
  it on host reload so the warm room survives. An idle room's DO Alarm sends `{kind:"evict"}` → browser maps it to
  `room:network-warning {reason:"room-evicted"}`. Deployment is APP-side (D26): Room ships no `wrangler.jsonc`; the
  app declares `ROOM_HUB`/`RATE_LIMIT`/`ASSETS` bindings and points `main` at its own `cloudflare/worker.ts`.
- **Host-reload recovery.** Host tab RELOAD is recoverable (host CRASH is an unmitigated v1 hard-failure). Host
  mints a `hostToken`, persists snapshots (debounced IndexedDB + sync localStorage on `visibilitychange`), resumes
  the same room code, verifies the token peer-side (no server, D6). Controllers buffer intents during absence and
  flush them (idempotent by `cSeq`, lossy by design). iOS degrades to "rescan QR" via `session.rejoin()`.
- **iOS caveats.** App-layer heartbeat is MANDATORY (WebKit DataChannel `onclose` doesn't fire on iOS). Controller
  Screen Wake Lock (Safari 16.4+) keeps the channel alive. Recovery degrades to rescan-QR.

## Facades (D19)

The two role facades (`stagePlugin`, `controllerPlugin`) are added to a client app via `createApp({ plugins: [...] })`
and RE-DECLARE all five `room:*` events so a game plugin with `depends: [stagePlugin]` / `[controllerPlugin]` sees the
full typed hook surface in one edge (event-type visibility is not transitive). They install NO forwarding hooks —
Moku's event bus is GLOBAL, so engines' `emit("room:*")` already reach every hook regardless of `depends`. Facades
delegate API methods only; they do not re-emit/proxy events, and own no config.

## Quick reference — compose (client core)

The four engines (`transport`, `session`, `intent`, `sync`) are core DEFAULTS — already wired. An app adds one role
facade + its game plugin; there are no role arrays.

```typescript
import { createApp, createPlugin, stagePlugin } from "@moku-labs/room";

const game = createPlugin("game", {
  depends: [stagePlugin], // makes the five room:* events visible
  hooks: ctx => ({ "room:peer-joined": ({ peerId }) => ctx.log.info(peerId) })
});

const app = createApp({ plugins: [stagePlugin, game] }); // engines are defaults; add facade + game
await app.start();

const { code, joinUrl } = app.stage.createRoom(); // SYNCHRONOUS — no await
const qr = await app.stage.qr();                  // QR is async (descriptor.qr is always null)
```

## Quick reference — role APIs

Reach the facade API by plugin name: `app.stage` (StageApi) / `app.controller` (ControllerApi). Inside a game
plugin: `ctx.require(stagePlugin)` / `ctx.require(controllerPlugin)`.

**StageApi (`app.stage`, host):**
- `createRoom(): RoomDescriptor` — SYNCHRONOUS; `{ code, joinUrl, qr, hostToken }` (`qr` is always `null` — fetch it
  via `qr()`).
- `qr(): Promise<QrMatrix | null>` — async QR matrix for the join URL (null when `generateQr: false`).
- `mutate(ns, recipe): void` — mutate one authoritative slice; recipe `(draft) => next`; schedules a throttled delta.
- `broadcast(): void` — force an immediate snapshot broadcast.
- `onIntent(name, handler): () => void` — handler `(payload, peerId)`; validated + de-duped before it runs.
- `roster(): readonly RosterEntry[]` — connected controllers.

**ControllerApi (`app.controller`, phone):**
- `joinRoom(code): Promise<void>` — passive join; THROWS `Error("full"|"not-found"|"unreachable")` on failure.
- `read(ns): Readonly<Record<string, JsonValue>> | undefined` — read the replica.
- `on(ns, cb): () => void` — subscribe to one namespace.
- `intent(name, payload): void` — send a typed input over the Wire (never emit); fire-and-forget.
- `requestWakeLock(): Promise<boolean>` / `releaseWakeLock(): Promise<void>` — iOS Screen Wake Lock.

## Quick reference — adapters

- `publicRendezvous()` — DEFAULT signaling adapter (Trystero v0.25.x over a Nostr backbone). Production, zero infra.
- `inMemory(options?)` — in-process adapter, no `RTCPeerConnection`. Tests/simulation; pair with `iceServers: []`.
  `inMemory({ server: true })` returns a server-mode adapter (persistent sessions; simulates join/peer-arrived/relay/
  reclaim/evict) so the server path is testable without a live DO.
- `serverSignaling(url)` — OPT-IN worker-backed adapter (one persistent WS to your `@moku-labs/room/server` Worker).
  Enables in-band discovery + host-reload reclaim. Lazy-loaded — bundles that never call it pay nothing.

```typescript
createApp({ plugins: [stagePlugin],
  pluginConfigs: { transport: { signaling: inMemory(), iceServers: [] } } });

// Server tier (browser side): widen the code space for the public endpoint.
createApp({ plugins: [stagePlugin],
  pluginConfigs: { transport: { signaling: serverSignaling("wss://room.example.com") }, session: { codeLength: 8 } } });
```

## Server core (`@moku-labs/room/server`)

The opt-in server core is its OWN `createCore` on the shared `coreConfig` with the `hubPlugin` default. Like every
Moku framework it EXPORTS `createApp` (never calls it) and exports NO `fetch` handler — a Layer-3 Cloudflare app
composes the app and wires the Worker itself (two files: a root `src/server.ts` that creates the app once, and a
`src/cloudflare/worker.ts` that delegates `fetch` to `app.hub.handle` and re-exports `Hub` for the binding).

```typescript
// your-app/src/server.ts — ROOT composition: create the app once, export it.
import { createApp } from "@moku-labs/room/server";
export const app = createApp(); // the `hub` is wired by default

// your-app/src/cloudflare/worker.ts — Cloudflare entry: USE the app to wire the Worker.
import { app } from "../server";
export { Hub } from "@moku-labs/room/server"; // re-export the DO class → wrangler binds ROOM_HUB
export default {
  fetch: (req: Request, env: Record<string, unknown>, ctx: ExecutionContext) => app.hub.handle(req, env, ctx)
} satisfies ExportedHandler;
```

## File structure

- `src/config.ts` — Step 1: `createCoreConfig("room", …)` (registers the `log`/`env` core plugins from
  `@moku-labs/common`) + the `RoomEvents` contract; exports the bound `createPlugin` (engines import it) + `createCore`.
- `src/index.ts` — the CLIENT core (`.`): `createCore` with the four engines as defaults + `browserEnv`; exports the
  bound `createApp` + `createPlugin`, the plugin instances, the signaling-adapter factories, and the protocol types.
- `src/server.ts` — the SERVER core (`./server`): `createCore` with `hubPlugin`; exports the bound `createApp` +
  `createPlugin`, the `hubPlugin` instance, and the `Hub` Durable Object class. No `@moku-labs/worker`; no `fetch`.
- `src/plugins/<name>/` — one dir per plugin: `index.ts` (wiring), `types.ts`, `config.ts`, `api.ts`, plus engine
  internals (`state.ts`, `lifecycle/`, `recovery/`, `codec.ts`, the transport `adapters/` + `protocol.ts`, …) and
  colocated `__tests__/`.
- `src/plugins/transport/protocol.ts` — the single physical home for the wire/signaling protocol: all shared types,
  the two consts, the `Frame` union, and the §1.3 `ClientEnvelope`/`ServerEnvelope` server-protocol unions.
- `src/plugins/hub/` — the opt-in server plugin: `api.ts` (`handle`), `hub-do.ts` (the `Hub` Durable Object,
  co-located — `defineDurableObject`, NOT a plugin), `sqlite.ts` (roster + reclaim tokens), `config.ts`, `state.ts`.

## Public export surface (from src/index.ts)

- Factories: `createApp` (the Layer-3 entry — wires the four engines by default), `createPlugin` (author a custom
  plugin bound to Room's `Config`/`Events`).
- Plugin instances: `transportPlugin`, `sessionPlugin`, `intentPlugin`, `syncPlugin`, `stagePlugin`,
  `controllerPlugin`.
- Signaling adapter factories: `publicRendezvous` (default), `inMemory` (tests), `serverSignaling` (opt-in, worker).
- Contract types (from `transport/protocol`): `Signaling`, `SignalingSession`, `SignalingJoinOpts`, `SignalMsg`,
  `IceCandidateInit`, `Wire`, `Frame`, `IntentFrame`, `SyncSnapshotFrame`, `SyncDeltaFrame`, `HeartbeatPingFrame`,
  `HeartbeatPongFrame`, `RecoveryHelloFrame`, `RecoveryWelcomeFrame`, `RecoveryFlushFrame`, `PeerId`, `RosterEntry`,
  `Snapshot`, `Op`, `JsonValue`, `Namespace`.
- Contract consts: `MAX_CONTROLLERS` (8), `ROOM_CODE_LENGTH` (6).
- Event contract: `RoomEvents` (from `src/config.ts`).
- Plugin-owned public types: `RoomDescriptor`, `JoinResult`, `QrMatrix` (session); `StageApi` (stage); `ControllerApi`
  (controller).
- NOTE: `ClientEnvelope`/`ServerEnvelope` (§1.3) are internal protocol types — defined in `transport/protocol.ts`,
  shared by `serverSignaling` + `inMemory({server:true})` + the DO, but NOT re-exported from the client barrel.

## Server core export surface (from src/server.ts — `@moku-labs/room/server`)

- `createApp` — the server core's Layer-3 entry; wires `hubPlugin` by default. `app.hub.handle(req, env, ctx)` is the
  request handler a consumer's `cloudflare/worker.ts` delegates its `fetch` to. (The framework exports it; it never
  calls it, and exports no `fetch`.)
- `createPlugin` — author a custom server plugin bound to Room's core.
- `hubPlugin` — the `hub` plugin instance (already a server-core default; exported for custom composition).
- `Hub` — the Durable Object class (re-export from your Worker entry so `wrangler` can bind `ROOM_HUB` to it).

## Plugins (one line each)

| Plugin | Tier | Wiring | Depends | Purpose | Key API | Events |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `transportPlugin` | Complex | client default | — | WebRTC floor: handshake, chunking/backpressure, heartbeat, capped ICE recovery; owns `Wire`. | `connect`, `wire`, `disconnect`, `peers`, `close` | emits `room:network-warning` |
| `sessionPlugin` | Complex | client default | transport | Room code + QR + roster; star topology (`hostId()`); host-reload recovery. | `createRoom`, `qr`, `joinRoom`, `leave`, `rejoin`, `roster`, `self`, `hostId`, `hostToken`, `persistSnapshot`, `recoveryPhase` | emits `room:peer-joined`, `room:peer-left`, `room:host-reconnecting` |
| `intentPlugin` | Standard | client default | transport, session | Controller→host typed inputs; per-controller `cSeq` idempotent de-dup; reconnect buffer. | `register`, `onIntent`, `intent`, `setBuffering`, `drainBuffer`, `bufferedCount` | none |
| `syncPlugin` | Complex | client default | transport, session | Host→controller authoritative state: snapshot + throttled op-list deltas (custom codec). | `registerSlice`, `mutate`, `broadcast`, `read`, `subscribe`, `applyFrame`, `exportSnapshot`, `importSnapshot` | emits `room:sync-ready` |
| `stagePlugin` | Standard (facade) | app-added (host) | all four engines | Host-role facade → `StageApi` (`app.stage`). | `createRoom`, `qr`, `mutate`, `broadcast`, `onIntent`, `roster` | re-declares all five |
| `controllerPlugin` | Standard (facade) | app-added (controller) | all four engines | Controller-role facade → `ControllerApi` (`app.controller`). | `joinRoom`, `read`, `on`, `intent`, `requestWakeLock`, `releaseWakeLock` | re-declares all five |
| `hubPlugin` | Standard | server-core default | native Cloudflare `env` (DO + KV) | OPT-IN operated signaling tier (`@moku-labs/room/server`); the only server-core plugin. WS-Hibernation `Hub` DO-per-room: handshake + in-band discovery + host-reload reclaim; NO gameplay relay (D2). | `handle(request, env, ctx)` | none |

## Events — `room:*` (Moku `emit`, coarse lifecycle ONLY)

ALL gameplay rides the `Wire` (`Frame`s), never these events.

- `room:peer-joined` → `{ peerId: PeerId }` — controller channel connected + added to roster (session).
- `room:peer-left` → `{ peerId: PeerId }` — controller left or heartbeat-declared dead (session).
- `room:host-reconnecting` → `{}` (`Record<string, never>`) — host reloaded, recovery in flight (session).
- `room:sync-ready` → `{}` — first full snapshot applied, replica readable (sync).
- `room:network-warning` → `{ reason: "ice-failed" | "rendezvous-unreachable" | "channel-closed" | "room-evicted" }`
  — connectivity hard-failure UX hook (transport, D2). `room-evicted` is server-tier only (`serverSignaling` got
  `{kind:"evict"}` from the DO's idle Alarm).

Reload-path caveat: `room:host-reconnecting` fires during `session` `onInit`, before downstream hooks exist. On the
reload path, POLL `app.session.recoveryPhase()` (non-`"stable"` = recovery in flight) instead of relying on it.

## Frames — `Frame` union (the typed `Wire`, gameplay transport, NOT events)

Discriminated on `t`. `Wire = { send(peerId, frame), broadcast(frame), on(handler) => unsubscribe }`. On a
controller, `send`/`broadcast` collapse to "send to host".

- `IntentFrame` (`t: "intent"`) — controller→host typed input; `{ name, payload, cSeq }`.
- `SyncSnapshotFrame` (`t: "sync-snap"`) — host→controller full snapshot; `{ snapshot, sSeq }`.
- `SyncDeltaFrame` (`t: "sync-delta"`) — host→controller op-list delta; `{ ops, sSeq }`.
- `HeartbeatPingFrame` (`t: "ping"`) / `HeartbeatPongFrame` (`t: "pong"`) — `{ ts }` liveness.
- `RecoveryHelloFrame` (`t: "recovery-hello"`) — controller→host re-entry claim; `{ hostToken, peerId }`.
- `RecoveryWelcomeFrame` (`t: "recovery-welcome"`) — host→controller ack; `{ hostToken, sSeq }`.
- `RecoveryFlushFrame` (`t: "recovery-flush"`) — controller→host buffered-intent flush; `{ buffered }`.
- `RosterFrame` (`t: "roster"`) — host→controller authoritative roster; `{ roster: { [peerId]: RosterEntry } }`.

## Server protocol envelopes — `ClientEnvelope`/`ServerEnvelope` (§1.3, server tier only)

Internal types (`transport/protocol.ts`; NOT client-barrel exports). The client↔DO WebSocket protocol for
`serverSignaling` + `inMemory({server:true})` + the `Hub` DO. Plain-JSON/DOM-free. The `relay` variants CARRY a
`SignalMsg` (the DO never inspects it — no gameplay relay, D2). Discriminated on `kind`.

- ClientEnvelope (client→DO): `join {selfId, role:"host"|"controller"}` · `reclaim {selfId, reclaimToken}` ·
  `relay {to, msg: SignalMsg}`.
- ServerEnvelope (DO→client): `join-ack {peers, reclaimToken}` · `peer-arrived {peerId, role}` · `peer-left {peerId}`
  · `reclaim-ack {peers}` · `relay {from, msg}` · `full` · `evict` · `error {code, message}`.

## Config defaults (override via createApp pluginConfigs)

- transport: `signaling=publicRendezvous()`, `iceServers=[one public STUN]`, `heartbeatIntervalMs=2000`,
  `heartbeatTimeoutMs=6000`, `openTimeoutMs=3000`, `maxMessageBytes=14336`.
- session: `joinUrlBase=""`, `generateQr=true`, `maxControllers=8`, `snapshotDebounceMs=500`,
  `reconnectTimeoutMs=10000`, `intentBufferMax=256`, `intentBufferMaxAgeMs=8000`, `storageKeyPrefix="moku.room"`,
  `codeLength=6` (set `8` for `serverSignaling` deployments — D24 room-code-enumeration resistance).
- intent: `bufferCap=256`, `bufferMaxAgeMs=10000`.
- sync: `broadcastHz=30` (clamped [5,60]), `skipEmptyDeltas=true`, `maxOpsPerDelta=512`, `resyncOnGap=true`.
- hub (server core, `pluginConfigs.hub`): `doBinding="ROOM_HUB"`, `doClassName="Hub"`, `assetsBinding="ASSETS"`,
  `rateLimit={joins:30,windowSec:60,kvBinding:"RATE_LIMIT"}`, `joinWindowMs=10000`, `roomTtlMs=1800000`.
- stage / controller: no config (facades own no tunables).

## End-to-end flow

1. Stage `app.stage.createRoom()` → `{ code, joinUrl, hostToken }` + `await app.stage.qr()`; render the QR. Register
   slices + intent handlers.
2. Controller scans QR, `await app.controller.joinRoom(code)`; `requestWakeLock()`.
3. Controller `app.controller.intent("move", {...})` → `IntentFrame` over the Wire → host.
4. Host `onIntent` validates + de-dups by `cSeq`, then `app.stage.mutate(ns, recipe)`.
5. Sync coalesces mutates at `broadcastHz` and broadcasts `SyncDeltaFrame`s; new/late joiners get a
   `SyncSnapshotFrame`.
6. Controller `app.controller.on(ns, cb)` re-renders on each applied frame; first snapshot → `room:sync-ready`.
7. Host reload: snapshot persisted → resume same code → token verified peer-side → controllers reconcile + flush
   buffered intents.

## Development

- Scripts (bun): `bun run build` (tsdown — client `.` + server `./server`), `bun run typecheck` (tsc ×2: client +
  worker tsconfig), `bun run lint` (Biome + ESLint), `bun run test` / `bun run test:unit` / `bun run test:integration`
  / `bun run test:coverage` (vitest, 90% threshold), `bun run test:e2e` / `bun run test:e2e:worker` (Playwright —
  manual gates), `bun run validate` (publint + attw), `bun run sandbox` / `bun run sandbox:worker` (manual tryout).
- Tests: framework-level in root `tests/unit/` + `tests/integration/`; plugin-specific colocated in
  `src/plugins/<name>/__tests__/`. Integration uses the `inMemory()` adapter; the worker e2e drives real `workerd`.
